title: Mechanism of Action of Grapes and Their Active Components. Polyphenolic compounds in grapes, such as resveratrol, can function as photoprotective agents that prevent or reduce ultraviolet-induced inflammation, oxidative stress, and D N A damage. Resveratrol is found in grape skin. When applied topically, it can act as a sunscreen and prevent ultraviolet B-induced skin cancer by absorbing ultraviolet B radiation. In addition, topical resveratrol also prevents the upregulation of cell cycle proteins and M A P K induced by ultraviolet B and upregulates the expression of p21 and p53.

In addition, topical resveratrol also prevents the upregulation of cell cycle proteins and M A P K induced by ultraviolet B and upregulates the expression of p21 and p53.
